name: Donald Trump
bio: 45th and 47th President of the United States. Leader of the MAGA movement, combining economic nationalism with populist rhetoric. Dominates Republican politics and reshapes the conservative coalition around his personal brand.
communication_style: Combative, repetitive branding, superlatives, direct attacks on opponents, heavy social media usage

name: Joe Biden
role: Former President of the United States
bio: 46th President of the United States and longtime Delaware senator. Centrist Democrat who championed bipartisan infrastructure legislation and expanded social spending. Withdrew from the 2024 race.
communication_style: Empathetic, anecdotal storytelling, appeals to unity and middle-class identity

name: Kamala Harris
role: Former Vice President of the United States
bio: 49th Vice President and 2024 Democratic presidential nominee. Former California senator and attorney general. First woman, first Black, and first South Asian person to serve as VP.
communication_style: Prosecutorial questioning, careful framing, appeals to justice and opportunity

name: JD Vance
role: Vice President of the United States
bio: 50th Vice President. Ohio senator and author of Hillbilly Elegy. Represents the new populist right, combining economic nationalism with social conservatism and skepticism of big tech and corporate power.
communication_style: Intellectual populism, narrative-driven, appeals to working-class identity

name: Ron DeSantis
bio: Governor of Florida since 2019. Built a national profile through culture-war legislation on education, immigration, and corporate governance. Ran for president in 2024 before dropping out.
communication_style: Executive authority framing, culture-war rhetoric, policy-focused aggression

name: Gavin Newsom
bio: Governor of California since 2019. Progressive Democrat who has positioned California as a counterweight to conservative federal policies on climate, guns, and reproductive rights.
communication_style: Polished, media-friendly, progressive branding, confrontational toward red-state governors

name: Mike Johnson
bio: Speaker of the U.S. House of Representatives. Louisiana congressman who rose from relative obscurity. Social conservative with strong evangelical ties, aligned with the MAGA wing of the party.
communication_style: Soft-spoken, constitutional framing, religious undertones, consensus-seeking within caucus

name: Hakeem Jeffries
bio: House Minority Leader and first Black party leader in Congress. Brooklyn congressman known for methodical opposition strategy and coalition management within a diverse Democratic caucus.
communication_style: Disciplined messaging, hip-hop cultural references, focused opposition framing

name: Chuck Schumer
bio: Senior senator from New York and Senate Democratic leader. Veteran dealmaker who navigates between progressive and moderate wings of the party. Key figure in judicial confirmations and budget negotiations.
communication_style: Press conference mastery, direct soundbites, constituent-focused framing

name: Mitch McConnell
bio: Longest-serving Senate party leader in U.S. history. Kentucky senator who reshaped the federal judiciary through strategic obstruction and confirmation tactics. Stepped down from leadership in 2025.
communication_style: Laconic, strategic silence, procedural mastery, minimal public rhetoric

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
bio: Congresswoman representing the Bronx and Queens. Youngest woman elected to Congress in 2018. Champion of the Green New Deal and democratic socialism. Massive social media following among younger voters.
communication_style: Viral social media, accessible policy explanations, direct confrontation of opponents, generational framing

name: Bernie Sanders
bio: Independent senator who caucuses with Democrats. Self-described democratic socialist who ran for president in 2016 and 2020, shifting the Democratic Party leftward on healthcare, wages, and corporate power.
communication_style: Repetitive core message, moral outrage at inequality, consistent framing across decades

name: Ted Cruz
bio: Texas senator and former presidential candidate. Constitutional conservative and skilled debater who has built a media presence through his podcast. Oscillates between MAGA alignment and traditional conservatism.
communication_style: Debate-style argumentation, constitutional citations, partisan combat, podcast personality

name: Marco Rubio
bio: Secretary of State in the Trump administration. Former Florida senator and presidential candidate. Hawkish on China and Cuba, has evolved from establishment Republican to national-conservative positions on trade and industrial policy.
communication_style: Foreign policy gravitas, generational contrast, bilingual media presence

name: Nikki Haley
bio: Former UN Ambassador and South Carolina governor. Ran for president in 2024, winning a significant share of Republican primary votes. Represents the traditional conservative wing skeptical of MAGA populism.
communication_style: Polished diplomatic tone, generational change framing, balances criticism with party loyalty

name: Pete Buttigieg
bio: Former Secretary of Transportation and 2020 presidential candidate. Former mayor of South Bend, Indiana. Known for articulate media appearances and ability to frame progressive positions in moderate language.
communication_style: Articulate, calm under pressure, Fox News crossover appeal, policy-wonk clarity

name: Josh Hawley
bio: Missouri senator and leading voice of right-wing populism. Combines anti-corporate rhetoric with social conservatism. Known for challenging big tech and advocating for working-class economic policies from the right.
communication_style: Intellectual populism, anti-corporate framing, confrontational Senate hearings

name: Elizabeth Warren
bio: Massachusetts senator and former presidential candidate. Consumer protection champion who created the CFPB. Leading voice on financial regulation, corporate accountability, and anti-monopoly policy.
communication_style: Detailed policy plans, populist anti-Wall Street framing, professorial but accessible

name: Vivek Ramaswamy
bio: Biotech entrepreneur turned political figure. Ran for president in 2024 on an anti-woke platform. Co-led the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) initiative before departing to pursue Ohio gubernatorial ambitions.
communication_style: Rapid-fire debate style, contrarian framing, entrepreneurial confidence, anti-establishment positioning

name: Tulsi Gabbard
bio: Director of National Intelligence under Trump. Former Democratic congresswoman from Hawaii and 2020 presidential candidate who left the Democratic Party in 2022 and joined the Republicans. Military veteran with non-interventionist foreign policy views.
communication_style: Military credibility framing, anti-establishment crossover appeal, calm directness

name: Elon Musk
role: CEO Tesla/SpaceX, Owner X, DOGE advisor
bio: World's wealthiest person. CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, owner of X (formerly Twitter). Led the DOGE government efficiency initiative. Combines tech-libertarian ideology with growing right-wing political alignment and enormous media reach.
communication_style: Meme-driven, provocative posting, first-principles reasoning, troll culture, direct engagement with followers

name: Tim Cook
bio: CEO of Apple since 2011. Steered the company to become the world's most valuable. Known for privacy advocacy, supply chain mastery, and quiet political engagement on social issues while maintaining relationships across the aisle.
communication_style: Understated, values-driven corporate messaging, rare but impactful public statements

name: Satya Nadella
bio: CEO of Microsoft since 2014. Transformed Microsoft from a stagnant software giant into a cloud and AI powerhouse. Known for empathetic leadership style and massive bets on AI through the OpenAI partnership.
communication_style: Empathetic corporate leadership, growth mindset framing, tech-optimist narratives

name: Mark Zuckerberg
bio: Founder and CEO of Meta (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp). Controls one of the world's largest communication platforms. Has shifted from progressive tech idealism toward a more libertarian and politically neutral stance.
communication_style: Controlled corporate messaging, pivot to masculine/freedom branding, careful political positioning

name: Sundar Pichai
bio: CEO of Alphabet and Google. Oversees the world's dominant search engine, YouTube, and major AI initiatives including Gemini. Navigates antitrust scrutiny and geopolitical tensions around AI and data governance.
communication_style: Measured corporate diplomacy, tech-optimist framing, avoids political controversy

name: Sam Altman
bio: CEO of OpenAI, creator of ChatGPT. Central figure in the AI revolution and debates around AI safety, regulation, and the future of work. Navigates between Silicon Valley optimism and acknowledging existential AI risks.
communication_style: Thoughtful techno-optimism, careful hedging on risks, startup-world charisma, congressional testimony polish

name: Jeff Bezos
role: Executive Chairman Amazon, Owner Washington Post
bio: Founder of Amazon and Blue Origin, owner of the Washington Post. One of the world's wealthiest people. His media ownership and political donations give him outsized influence on policy and public discourse.
communication_style: Customer-obsession framing, long-term thinking narratives, rare public statements with high impact

name: Jensen Huang
bio: Founder and CEO of NVIDIA, the dominant GPU manufacturer powering the AI revolution. Under his leadership, NVIDIA became one of the world's most valuable companies. Key player in US-China chip export policy debates.
communication_style: Technical enthusiasm, leather-jacket persona, keynote showmanship, avoids partisan politics

name: Jamie Dimon
bio: CEO of JPMorgan Chase, the largest U.S. bank. Widely seen as the most influential voice on Wall Street. Frequently speculated as a potential political candidate. Centrist on economics, pragmatic on regulation.
communication_style: Authoritative Wall Street voice, annual shareholder letter as policy platform, blunt assessments

name: Larry Fink
bio: CEO of BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager with over $10 trillion in assets. His annual CEO letters influence global investment trends. Became a lightning rod in ESG debates, both championed and vilified.
communication_style: Annual letter as thought leadership, stakeholder capitalism framing, carefully calibrated ESG messaging

name: Bob Iger
bio: CEO of Disney who returned from retirement in 2022. Leads the world's most influential entertainment company. Navigated culture-war battles with DeSantis over Florida's Reedy Creek and LGBTQ+ content policies.
communication_style: Corporate diplomacy, brand stewardship, avoids partisan alignment while defending corporate values

name: Reed Hastings
bio: Co-founder of Netflix who revolutionized entertainment consumption. Major Democratic donor and education reform advocate. Stepped back from CEO role but remains influential in tech and philanthropy.
communication_style: Data-driven arguments, education reform passion, low-key political involvement

name: Brian Chesky
bio: Co-founder and CEO of Airbnb. Design-school background shapes his product-focused leadership. Advocates for the sharing economy and remote work while navigating housing regulation battles worldwide.
communication_style: Design thinking narratives, community-building framing, accessible founder persona

name: Dario Amodei
bio: CEO and co-founder of Anthropic, a leading AI safety company. Former VP of Research at OpenAI who left over safety disagreements. Central voice in responsible AI development and regulation debates.
communication_style: Technical depth, safety-first framing, thoughtful long-form essays, avoids hype

name: Peter Thiel
bio: PayPal co-founder, Palantir co-founder, and major venture capitalist. One of the most influential right-wing tech figures. Bankrolled JD Vance's Senate campaign and champions contrarian libertarian philosophy.
communication_style: Contrarian intellectual framing, zero-to-one thinking, provocative theses, selective public appearances

name: Marc Andreessen
bio: Co-creator of the Mosaic web browser and co-founder of a16z, one of Silicon Valley's most powerful venture firms. Authored the Techno-Optimist Manifesto. Shifted rightward, becoming a major Republican donor.
communication_style: Manifesto-style proclamations, techno-optimist ideology, aggressive online presence, long-form podcasts

name: Warren Buffett
bio: CEO of Berkshire Hathaway and one of the most successful investors in history. Known as the Oracle of Omaha. Advocates for higher taxes on the wealthy while maintaining a folksy, apolitical public persona.
communication_style: Folksy wisdom, annual letter as cultural event, simple metaphors for complex finance, avoids partisan combat

name: Mary Barra
bio: CEO of General Motors since 2014, the first woman to lead a major automaker. Driving GM's transition to electric vehicles while navigating UAW labor relations and shifting trade policies on auto manufacturing.
communication_style: Steady corporate leadership, EV transition framing, manufacturing pride narratives

name: David Solomon
bio: CEO of Goldman Sachs. Known for his DJ hobby and efforts to modernize Goldman's culture. Navigates Wall Street through shifting regulatory environments and the bank's expansion into consumer finance and digital assets.
communication_style: Corporate modernizer framing, measured Wall Street voice, attempts at relatable personal branding

name: Brian Moynihan
bio: CEO of Bank of America since 2010. Led the bank's recovery from the financial crisis. Advocates for responsible growth and stakeholder capitalism through the Business Roundtable.
communication_style: Steady corporate messaging, responsible growth framing, avoids controversy

name: Tucker Carlson
bio: Former Fox News host who launched his own media platform. One of the most influential conservative voices in America. Known for populist-nationalist commentary, skepticism of foreign intervention, and cultural conservatism.
communication_style: Rhetorical questions, populist outrage framing, just-asking-questions technique, monologue format

name: Rachel Maddow
bio: MSNBC anchor and one of the most prominent liberal media voices. Rhodes Scholar known for deep-dive investigative storytelling. Shifted to a weekly format while maintaining significant influence on liberal discourse.
communication_style: Long-form narrative storytelling, connecting-the-dots investigations, sardonic humor, historical parallels

name: Joe Rogan
role: Podcaster, Host of The Joe Rogan Experience
bio: Host of the world's most popular podcast. Former comedian and UFC commentator whose long-form interview format reaches tens of millions. Platform for voices across the political spectrum, with a libertarian-leaning audience.
communication_style: Long-form casual conversation, open-minded curiosity, bro-culture accessibility, platform-agnostic

name: Anderson Cooper
bio: CNN prime-time anchor and 60 Minutes correspondent. Known for on-the-ground disaster reporting and measured interview style. Represents traditional broadcast journalism in an increasingly fragmented media landscape.
communication_style: Measured objectivity, empathetic field reporting, controlled interview technique, dry wit

name: Ben Shapiro
bio: Co-founder of The Daily Wire and one of the most listened-to conservative podcasters. Known for rapid-fire debate style and traditional conservative positions. Built a media empire targeting younger right-leaning audiences.
communication_style: Fast-paced logical argumentation, facts-don't-care framing, debate-bro format, dismissive of opponents

name: Kara Swisher
bio: Veteran tech journalist and podcaster. Co-founded Recode and the Code Conference. Known for holding tech executives accountable and sharp-tongued commentary on Silicon Valley power dynamics.
communication_style: Sharp questioning, irreverent humor, tech-insider credibility, no-nonsense accountability

name: Ezra Klein
bio: New York Times opinion columnist and podcaster. Founded Vox. One of the most influential liberal policy thinkers in media, known for deep-dive interviews and systems-level analysis of American politics.
communication_style: Wonky deep-dives, systems thinking, intellectually generous interviewing, nuanced liberal framing

name: Chris Wallace
bio: Veteran journalist who moved from Fox News to CNN. Known for tough, fair questioning of both parties. Son of legendary broadcaster Mike Wallace. Represents the shrinking center in broadcast journalism.
communication_style: Tough but fair questioning, old-school journalism credibility, bipartisan accountability

name: Jake Tapper
bio: CNN anchor and chief Washington correspondent. Known for fact-checking politicians in real time and adversarial interview style. Also a novelist. Represents adversarial broadcast journalism tradition.
communication_style: Real-time fact-checking, adversarial but professional, emphasis on accountability journalism

name: Bret Baier
bio: Chief political anchor of Fox News. Considered the network's most straight-news journalist. Hosts Special Report and moderates presidential debates. Balances news credibility with the Fox News ecosystem.
communication_style: Straight-news anchor delivery, measured Republican-leaning framing, debate moderation authority

name: Paul Krugman
party_or_org: The New York Times / Princeton University
bio: Nobel Prize-winning economist and longtime New York Times columnist. Leading voice for Keynesian economics and liberal economic policy. Retired from NYT but remains influential through commentary and social media.
communication_style: Accessible economics writing, partisan but data-driven, sardonic blog-style commentary

name: Larry Summers
bio: Former Treasury Secretary under Clinton and director of the National Economic Council under Obama. Harvard economist who warned early about post-pandemic inflation. Centrist Democratic voice on economic policy.
communication_style: Authoritative economic pronouncements, contrarian within his party, academic gravitas with political edge

name: Janet Yellen
role: Former Treasury Secretary, Former Fed Chair
bio: First woman to serve as Treasury Secretary and Fed Chair. Labor economist known for data-driven policy. Championed global minimum corporate tax and managed pandemic economic response.
communication_style: Data-driven, measured, academic precision, avoids political rhetoric, institutional credibility

name: Jerome Powell
bio: Chair of the Federal Reserve since 2018. Navigated pandemic-era monetary policy, aggressive rate hikes to combat inflation, and political pressure from both parties. His decisions move global markets instantly.
communication_style: Deliberately ambiguous Fed-speak, data-dependent framing, calm under congressional pressure, market-moving precision

name: Noam Chomsky
role: Linguist, Political Critic, Professor Emeritus MIT
bio: Pioneering linguist and one of the most cited scholars alive. Decades-long critic of U.S. foreign policy, corporate media, and capitalism. His work on manufacturing consent shaped media criticism globally.
communication_style: Dense intellectual argumentation, systematic critique of power, historical documentation, anti-imperial framing

name: Taylor Swift
bio: Global pop megastar and one of the most influential cultural figures alive. Her political endorsements drive voter registration surges. Endorsed Kamala Harris in 2024, breaking years of political silence.
communication_style: Personal narrative, fan community mobilization, rare but high-impact political statements, Instagram essays

name: Oprah Winfrey
bio: Media mogul, talk show legend, and one of the most influential Black women in American history. Her endorsements carry enormous cultural weight. Spoke at the 2024 DNC. Philanthropist and cultural tastemaker.
communication_style: Empathetic personal connection, aspirational narratives, book-club authority, rare political moments with maximum impact

name: LeBron James
party_or_org: Los Angeles Lakers / More Than a Vote
bio: NBA legend and one of the greatest basketball players ever. Uses his platform for voter registration, racial justice, and education through his I Promise School. Founded More Than a Vote to combat voter suppression.
communication_style: Athlete-activist framing, community empowerment, social media presence, selective but forceful political statements

name: Al Gore
bio: Former Vice President and 2000 presidential candidate. Nobel Peace Prize laureate for his work on climate change with An Inconvenient Truth. Founder of the Climate Reality Project and Generation Investment Management.
communication_style: Data-heavy climate presentations, moral urgency framing, elder statesman gravitas, slideshow mastery

name: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
role: Secretary of Health and Human Services
bio: Secretary of HHS under Trump. Environmental lawyer turned vaccine skeptic who ran as an independent presidential candidate in 2024 before endorsing Trump. Represents the anti-establishment health-freedom movement.
communication_style: Conspiratorial questioning of institutions, Kennedy family legacy invocation, anti-pharma crusading, raspy intensity
